name: Albufera Loop Trail
name_original: Parque Natural de L'Albufera — Paseo circular desde Massanassa
description: Cycle a moderate 27.8-mile (44.8 km) circular route through Parque Natural de L'Albufera, enjoying rice fields, marshes, and lagoon views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Massanassa benefits from generally flat terrain and an extensive network of routes. The area is characterized by its proximity to L'Albufera Natural Park, featuring wetlands, rice fields, and lagoon views. Coastal paths along dunes and beaches are also accessible, providing varied cycling experiences. For more challenging rides, the Sierra Calderona Natural Park offers routes with varied elevations and mountain passes. Best touring cycling routes around Massanassa The most popular touring cycling route is Albufera Loop Trail, a 27.8 miles (44.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route offers an immersive experience through L'Albufera Natural Park's wetlands and rice fields. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Pujol Pond – Devesa del Saler Dunes loop from Massanassa, a moderate 47.5 miles (76.4 km) path. This route leads along coastal dunes and offers views of various beaches. Local touring cyclists also love the Views of the marsh – Casa de la Mata de Malta loop from Massanassa, a 30.5 miles (49.1 km) trail leading through marsh views, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Touring cycling around Massanassa is defined by flat coastal paths, the natural wetlands of L'Albufera, and access to the varied elevations of Sierra Calderona. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Massanassa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Massanassa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Massanassa?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Massanassa documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Massanassa?
A: Massanassa offers diverse terrain for touring cyclists. You'll find generally flat coastal paths and routes through the wetlands of L'Albufera Natural Park. For those seeking more challenge, the nearby Sierra Calderona Natural Park provides varied elevations and mountain passes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners around Massanassa?
A: Yes, Massanassa has several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23868090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;South Ring Road Bike Lane – Mural "All with one voice" loop from Massanassa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option, covering about 14.6 km with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of significant natural features. You can explore the wetlands and rice fields of L'Albufera Natural Park, with highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2439037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/404607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond&lt;/a&gt;. Coastal routes also feature the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/977301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bicycle lane along the dunes&lt;/a&gt; and beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/612932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Creu Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Massanassa are circular. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e916534313/albufera-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.8 km route through L'Albufera Natural Park. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33749346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pujol Pond – Devesa del Saler Dunes loop from Massanassa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 76.4 km path along coastal dunes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Massanassa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Massanassa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the serene wetlands of L'Albufera to the scenic coastal paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Massanassa?
A: The region around Massanassa generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures. Summer can be warm, but coastal routes provide refreshing sea breezes.

Q: Can I access historical sites or urban attractions by bike from Massanassa?
A: Yes, Massanassa's proximity to Valencia City means you can easily access its extensive network of cycle paths. These routes connect historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3420309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Virgen&lt;/a&gt; and modern architectural marvels such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2124622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City of Arts and Sciences&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of marshes or wetlands?
A: Absolutely. The area is known for its wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17307134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the marsh – Casa de la Mata de Malta loop from Massanassa&lt;/a&gt; is a 49.1 km trail specifically designed to showcase marsh views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21549866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the marsh – Camí Vell de Russafa loop from Massanassa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach cycling routes from Massanassa?
A: Massanassa is well-connected within the Valencia region, making it feasible to use public transport to access various starting points for cycling routes. Valencia's extensive cycle path network also integrates well with its public transport system, allowing for flexible travel with your bike.

Q: Are there opportunities for more challenging touring cycling rides?
A: While many routes are relatively flat, for more challenging rides with varied elevations, you can head towards the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. This area, often called Valencia's 'green lung,' offers mountain passes and more demanding terrain for experienced touring cyclists.
tips: Very picturesque and quiet site with the typical barges used for a long time. With a beautiful wooden walkway next to the ideal canal to walk an afternoon and watch the sunset
It is a picturesque path along the west side of the Albufera. Its trajectory coincides with part of the Via Augusta and the Camino del Cid, two well-known cycling routes.

It facilitates the connection between Sollana and El Palmar to make a short detour to the perimeter of the Albufera, adding the Camí Vell de Russafa to the west and the route parallel to the coast between Pinedo and El Saler.
Beautiful views of the rice fields and the natural park of La Albufera. It is a nice place to walk with the family, especially to enjoy the sunsets.

Perhaps one of the most photographed points outside the urban area of Valencia. This beautiful place offers beautiful panoramic views of the entire Albufera, its landscape of flora and fauna and is undoubtedly the best place to watch the sunsets at any time of the year.

In this area it is easy to see different species of marsh birds: blue heron, ibis, ducks, herons, etc.
In the Ravisanxo canal you can also enjoy the fast flight of the cornflower.

faqs.question: Where does this cycling route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Massanassa. While specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, Massanassa is a well-connected town near Valencia, and you can typically find public parking options within the town. Consider checking local parking availability upon arrival.

faqs.question: How long does this cycling route take?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 44.8 km (27.8 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. It features minimal elevation gain, traversing a predominantly flat landscape. While much of the surface is paved and suitable for touring bikes, some segments may be unpaved and more challenging, potentially requiring cyclists to dismount and push their bikes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a varied terrain, primarily consisting of paved surfaces suitable for touring bicycles. However, be prepared for some unpaved sections that might be more challenging. The route is largely flat, with very little elevation change.

faqs.question: What are the main sights or highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers numerous points of interest, including the Port of Catarroja, the scenic Camí Vell de Russafa, and the tranquil Marsh between Sollana and El Palmar. You'll also find excellent panoramic views from the Albufera Viewpoint and the Gola de Pujol Pier – Sunset Viewpoint. Don't miss the Birdwatching area for wildlife spotting.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling, and you can enjoy the natural beauty of the park without the intense summer heat.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: While the route is largely flat, its moderate difficulty and some potentially unpaved sections mean it might be best suited for families with older children who are comfortable with longer rides and varied terrain. Younger children or those less experienced might find parts challenging.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural de L'Albufera — Circular walk from Massanassa dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in the Parque Natural de l'Albufera, it's important to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife, especially birds. Always clean up after your pet. Specific regulations for cycling with dogs on this particular route may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parc Natural de l'Albufera?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Parc Natural de l'Albufera. The park is a protected natural area, and access for activities like cycling is generally free. However, always respect the park's regulations regarding conservation and visitor conduct.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other notable paths. Significant portions of it coincide with the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana and the GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT. You'll also find sections that are part of the Camino de Santiago de Levante and La Defensa del Sur.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Parque Natural de l'Albufera, particularly in nearby villages like El Palmar, offers opportunities to experience local culture and cuisine. You can find restaurants serving traditional Valencian dishes, such as paella, and there are various accommodation options in the surrounding towns and in Valencia itself.
